 {"id":"brenner_bot-o7a","title":"Testing Infrastructure Epic","description":"Comprehensive testing infrastructure for brenner_bot project.

## Scope
This epic covers all testing needs:
- Unit testing with Vitest (Bun-compatible, no mocks philosophy)
- E2E testing with Playwright (full browser automation)
- Detailed logging and test reporting
- CI/CD integration

## Philosophy
- NO mocks or fakes - test real behavior
- Use real implementations, real data fixtures
- Detailed logging for debugging test failures
- Tests should be documentation

## Components to Test
- Web app: lib/ (parsers, auth, corpus-search, agent-mail-client)
- Web app: components (UI primitives, page components)
- Web app: pages and routes
- CLI: brenner.ts (argument parsing, mail commands)
- E2E: Full user flows through the application

## Success Criteria
- All critical paths have unit coverage
- E2E tests cover happy paths and error cases
- Tests run in CI on every PR
- Test failures have clear, actionable output","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-29T22:09:00.851985604-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-29T23:43:47.468037506-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-29T23:43:47.468037506-05:00","close_reason":"Testing Infrastructure Epic complete. All children closed: Unit tests for libraries, component tests, CLI tests, E2E tests, and CI/CD integration."}

+{"id":"brenner_bot-oful","title":"Test Logging: Structured Logging for Unit Tests","description":"# Test Logging: Unit Test Structured Logging

## Current State
- E2E tests have excellent logging (e2e-logging.ts, network-logging.ts)
- Unit tests use console.log or nothing
- CLI tests capture stdout/stderr but no structured logging

## Goal
Port E2E logging patterns to unit tests for better debugging.

## Implementation

### 1. Create Unit Test Logger
**File:** `apps/web/src/test-utils/test-logger.ts`

```typescript
import { vi } from "vitest";

export enum LogLevel {
  DEBUG = 0,
  INFO = 1,
  STEP = 2,
  WARN = 3,
  ERROR = 4
}

interface LogEntry {
  timestamp: string;
  level: LogLevel;
  category: string;
  message: string;
  data?: unknown;
  stack?: string;
}

export class TestLogger {
  private entries: LogEntry[] = [];
  private minLevel: LogLevel;
  
  constructor(minLevel: LogLevel = LogLevel.INFO) {
    this.minLevel = minLevel;
  }
  
  debug(category: string, message: string, data?: unknown): void;
  info(category: string, message: string, data?: unknown): void;
  step(message: string): void;
  warn(category: string, message: string, data?: unknown): void;
  error(category: string, message: string, error?: Error): void;
  
  getEntries(): LogEntry[];
  getEntriesForCategory(category: string): LogEntry[];
  clear(): void;
  
  // For attaching to test reports
  toJSON(): object;
  toText(): string;
}

// Vitest afterEach hook to attach logs
export function attachLogsOnFailure(logger: TestLogger): void {
  afterEach(function() {
    if (this.currentTest?.state === "failed") {
      console.log("\n=== Test Logs ===\n");
      console.log(logger.toText());
    }
    logger.clear();
  });
}
```

### 2. Create Test Categories
```typescript
export const LogCategories = {
  API_CALL: "api",
  STORAGE: "storage",
  PARSE: "parse",
  VALIDATE: "validate",
  FIXTURE: "fixture",
  ASSERTION: "assert"
} as const;
```

### 3. Integration with Test Setup
**File:** `apps/web/src/test-setup.tsx` (modify existing)

```typescript
import { TestLogger, attachLogsOnFailure } from "./test-utils/test-logger";

// Create global logger instance
export const testLogger = new TestLogger();

// Auto-attach logs on failure
attachLogsOnFailure(testLogger);

// Make available globally
(globalThis as any).__testLogger = testLogger;
```

### 4. Usage in Tests
```typescript
import { testLogger, LogCategories } from "@/test-setup";

test("parses delta with multiple sections", () => {
  testLogger.step("Parsing delta with hypothesis and test sections");
  
  const input = `...`;
  testLogger.debug(LogCategories.PARSE, "Input delta", { length: input.length });
  
  const result = extractValidDeltas(input);
  testLogger.info(LogCategories.PARSE, "Parsed deltas", { count: result.length });
  
  expect(result).toHaveLength(2);
  testLogger.step("Assertion passed: 2 deltas extracted");
});
```

### 5. Network Call Logging
```typescript
// Wrap fetch in tests to log all calls
export function createLoggingFetch(logger: TestLogger): typeof fetch {
  return async (input, init) => {
    const url = typeof input === "string" ? input : input.url;
    logger.info(LogCategories.API_CALL, `Fetch: ${init?.method || "GET"} ${url}`);
    
    const start = performance.now();
    const response = await fetch(input, init);
    const duration = performance.now() - start;
    
    logger.info(LogCategories.API_CALL, `Response: ${response.status}`, {
      duration: `${duration.toFixed(0)}ms`,
      contentType: response.headers.get("content-type")
    });
    
    return response;
  };
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] TestLogger class implemented with all log levels
- [ ] Logs automatically attached on test failure
- [ ] Network logging helper created
- [ ] At least 3 test files updated to use logger
- [ ] Documentation added for logging patterns

## Files to Create/Modify
- `apps/web/src/test-utils/test-logger.ts` (create)
- `apps/web/src/test-utils/test-logger.test.ts` (create)
- `apps/web/src/test-setup.tsx` (modify)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-01T20:59:38.665276131-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-01T20:59:38.665276131-05:00"}

### Performance

- [ ] Dictionary lookup is fast

### Edge Cases

- [ ] Very long definitions don't break layout
- [ ] Special characters in terms (⊘, 𝓛) display correctly
- [ ] Nested jargon (term in term definition) handled

## Dependencies

 {"id":"brenner_bot-ox3","title":"EPIC: Brenner Jargon & Glossary System (tooltips + glossary page)","description":"# Brenner Jargon & Glossary System

## Overview
Implement a comprehensive jargon/glossary system that makes technical terms accessible to newcomers without boring experienced users. Inspired by the agentic_coding_flywheel_setup implementation but adapted to BrennerBot's design system and domain.

## The Problem
BrennerBot contains extensive technical vocabulary across multiple domains:
- **Brenner-specific operators** (⊘ Level-split, 𝓛 Recode, etc.)
- **Scientific methodology** (decision experiments, forbidden patterns)
- **Biology/genetics** (C. elegans, DNA, genetic code)
- **Bayesian reasoning** (likelihood ratio, prior/posterior)
- **Project terminology** (Agent Mail, beads, corpus)

A "normal educated person" unfamiliar with molecular biology, Brenner's work, or Bayesian statistics will encounter hundreds of terms they don't understand. This creates a barrier to entry and limits the project's accessibility.

## The Solution
A two-part system:

### 1. Inline Jargon Component (`<Jargon>`)
- **Desktop**: Hover triggers tooltip with short definition, "Think of it like..." analogy, and link to glossary
- **Mobile**: Tap triggers bottom sheet with full definition, why-we-use-it, and related terms
- **Styling**: Subtle dotted underline (matches brennerbot design system's primary/30 color)
- **Accessibility**: Proper ARIA, focus management, keyboard navigation

### 2. Glossary Page (`/glossary`)
- Searchable full-text index of all terms
- Category filtering (operators, biology, methodology, etc.)
- Deep linking via hash (#machine-language)
- Expandable detail cards with:
  - Short one-liner
  - Full explanation in plain language
  - "Think of it like..." analogy
  - "Why we use it" context
  - Related terms with links

## Design Constraints
- Must match existing BrennerBot design system (Stripe/Apple/Linear inspired)
- Use existing Tailwind tokens: `primary`, `accent`, `muted-foreground`, `rounded-xl`, etc.
- No external animation libraries (use existing CSS keyframes from globals.css)
- Mobile-first responsive design
- Support reduced-motion preferences
- NO MOCKS in tests - test with real glossary data

## Term Categories
1. **Brenner Operators** (14): ⊘ ⊕ ◊ 𝓛 ≡ ✂ ⟂ ↑ ΔE ∿ † ⌂ 🔧 ⊞
2. **Core Brenner Concepts** (6): Brenner move, decision experiment, digital handle, etc.
3. **Extended Brenner Vocabulary** (17): Abundance trick, Gedanken organism, Occam's broom, etc.
4. **Scientific Biology** (20+): DNA, RNA, C. elegans, genetic code, mutagenesis, etc.
5. **Bayesian/Statistical** (10+): Prior, posterior, likelihood ratio, KL divergence, etc.
6. **Scientific Method** (10+): Hypothesis, falsification, paradigm, control, etc.
7. **Project-Specific** (10+): Agent Mail, beads, corpus, kickoff, thread protocol, etc.

## Reference Implementation
Analyzed from github.com/Dicklesworthstone/agentic_coding_flywheel_setup:
- `lib/jargon.ts`: JargonTerm interface + dictionary
- `components/jargon.tsx`: Jargon component with portal tooltips
- `app/glossary/page.tsx`: Full glossary page with search/filter

## Success Criteria
- Any dotted-underline term can be hovered/tapped to learn its meaning
- Glossary is discoverable via navigation and tooltip links
- First-time users can understand content without external research
- Expert users are not annoyed by the glossary system
- All terms are accurate and grounded in project documentation","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-30T00:56:16.505719259-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T01:27:54.789921827-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T01:27:54.789921827-05:00","close_reason":"EPIC COMPLETE: Brenner Jargon & Glossary System fully implemented. 105 terms across 6 categories, Jargon component with tooltips/sheets, /glossary page with search/filter/deep-link, 66+ tests covering all functionality.","labels":["accessibility","glossary","ux"]}

 {"id":"brenner_bot-ucmp","title":"Task: Add State Transition CLI Commands","description":"# Task: Add State Transition CLI Commands

## Objective
Add CLI commands for triggering state transitions and viewing transition history.

## Implementation Details

### Commands

#### `brenner hypothesis activate <id>`
```bash
brenner hypothesis activate H-RS20251230-001

# Output:
✓ Activated hypothesis H-RS20251230-001
  State: proposed → active
```

#### `brenner hypothesis kill <id>`
```bash
brenner hypothesis kill H-RS20251230-001 \
  --test=T-RS20251230-002 \
  --reason="ATAC-seq showed open chromatin"

# Output:
✓ Killed hypothesis H-RS20251230-001
  State: active → killed
  Killed by: T-RS20251230-002
  Reason: ATAC-seq showed open chromatin
```

#### `brenner hypothesis refine <id>`
```bash
brenner hypothesis refine H-RS20251230-001 \
  --child=H-RS20251231-001

# Output:
✓ Refined hypothesis H-RS20251230-001
  State: active → refined
  Replaced by: H-RS20251231-001
```

#### `brenner hypothesis validate <id>`
```bash
brenner hypothesis validate H-RS20251230-001 \
  --test=T-RS20251230-003

# Output:
✓ Validated hypothesis H-RS20251230-001
  State: active → validated
  Validated by: T-RS20251230-003
  ⚠️  Note: Validation is provisional. New tests may still kill this hypothesis.
```

#### `brenner hypothesis park <id>`
```bash
brenner hypothesis park H-RS20251230-002 \
  --reason="Waiting for funding for lineage tracing experiment"

# Output:
✓ Parked hypothesis H-RS20251230-002
  State: active → dormant
  Reason: Waiting for funding for lineage tracing experiment
```

#### `brenner hypothesis reactivate <id>`
```bash
brenner hypothesis reactivate H-RS20251230-002

# Output:
✓ Reactivated hypothesis H-RS20251230-002
  State: dormant → active
```

#### `brenner hypothesis history <id>`
```bash
brenner hypothesis history H-RS20251230-001

# Output:
Timeline for H-RS20251230-001:
  2025-12-30T10:00:00Z  created (proposed)
  2025-12-30T10:05:00Z  activated
                        proposed → active
  2025-12-31T15:30:00Z  killed by T-RS20251230-002
                        active → killed
                        Reason: ATAC-seq showed open chromatin

Current state: killed (terminal)
```

### Error Handling
```bash
brenner hypothesis kill H-RS20251230-001

# Error (missing --test):
✗ Error: Kill transition requires --test=<test-id>
  Hypotheses should be killed by specific test results, not arbitrary decisions.
  Use --reason to explain if no test ID is available.
```

```bash
brenner hypothesis activate H-RS20251230-001
# (if already killed)

# Error (invalid transition):
✗ Error: Cannot activate hypothesis in state 'killed'
  Killed hypotheses are terminal. They cannot be resurrected.
  Consider creating a new hypothesis instead.
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All 7 commands implemented
- [ ] Required flags enforced (--test for kill, --child for refine)
- [ ] Clear error messages for invalid transitions
- [ ] History command shows full timeline
- [ ] Confirmation prompts for destructive actions (kill)
- [ ] `--yes` flag to skip confirmations

## Dependencies
- Task: Implement Transition Validator
- Task: Create Transition History Logging

## Effort Estimate
~250 lines of code, ~3 hours","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:45:06.785846836-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-31T12:09:32.801478171-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-31T12:09:32.801478171-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented all 7 CLI commands: activate, kill/refute, refine/supersede, validate/confirm, park/defer, reactivate, and history. All commands support --json mode, enforce required flags (--test for kill/validate, --child for refine), and log transitions to TransitionHistoryStore. 136 tests pass.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-ucmp","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-6n4e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:46:53.203760638-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-ucmp","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-a0r7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:46:53.360858756-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-ucmp","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-v41w","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:46:53.392165625-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-ucmp","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-jcuz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-30T22:41:13.587753337-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"brenner_bot-ueu","title":"Add debounce to QuoteBank search","description":"","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-29T23:14:29.387920975-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T00:20:28.134309693-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T00:20:28.134309693-05:00","close_reason":"Added 200ms debounce to QuoteBank search using useDebounce hook"}
 {"id":"brenner_bot-uk5","title":"Implement global search with command palette","description":"# Task

Create a global search experience using a command palette (Cmd+K / Ctrl+K).

# Why Command Palette

- Familiar UX pattern (VS Code, Slack, Notion, etc.)
- Fast access from anywhere
- Can search across all content types
- Can also trigger actions (not just search)

# Search Scope

Search across:
1. **Transcript sections** - by text content
2. **Distillations** - by text content
3. **Quote bank** - by quote text
4. **Operators** - by name or definition
5. **Pages** - by title (Corpus, Distillations, Method, etc.)

# UI

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  🔍 Search or type a command...                    ⌘K       │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│  RECENT                                                      │
│  📄 §42: Choice of experimental object                      │
│  📄 §87: Abundance trick                                    │
│                                                              │
│  SUGGESTIONS                                                 │
│  📄 Corpus → complete_brenner_transcript.md                 │
│  📊 Distillations → Compare models                          │
│  ⚡ Method → Operator palette                               │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

# Implementation

Use `cmdk` library (https://cmdk.paco.me/) - lightweight, accessible, customizable.

```tsx
import { Command } from 'cmdk';

<Command>
  <Command.Input placeholder='Search or type a command...' />
  <Command.List>
    <Command.Group heading='Recent'>
      {recentItems.map(item => (
        <Command.Item key={item.id}>{item.title}</Command.Item>
      ))}
    </Command.Group>
    // ...
  </Command.List>
</Command>
```

# Search Index

Build search index at build time:
1. Parse all markdown files
2. Extract searchable content
3. Store as JSON
4. Use client-side fuzzy search (fuse.js or similar)

# Keyboard Shortcuts

- `Cmd+K` / `Ctrl+K` - Open search
- `Escape` - Close
- `↑↓` - Navigate results
- `Enter` - Go to result
- `Cmd+Enter` - Open in new tab

# Actions (Beyond Search)

Command palette can also:
- 'Go to Corpus' → navigate
- 'Toggle dark mode' → action
- 'Start new session' → navigate + action

# Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Command palette opens with Cmd+K
- [ ] Search returns relevant results
- [ ] Results grouped by type
- [ ] Recent searches shown
- [ ] Keyboard navigation works
- [ ] Actions work (navigation, etc.)
- [ ] Accessible","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:22:55.026628542-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T13:41:09.589330231-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T13:41:09.589330231-05:00","close_reason":"Already implemented via SpotlightSearch.tsx - Cmd+K command palette with search across transcript, quotes, distillations, operators; keyboard navigation, actions, and accessibility","labels":["command-palette","search","ux","web"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-uk5","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-8yn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:23:07.039014299-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-uk5","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so.4.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:28:02.327651525-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"brenner_bot-uwe","title":"Task: Implement result highlighting","description":"Highlight matched terms in search results.

## Purpose

Highlighting shows users WHY a result matched, helping them quickly identify relevant results.

## Requirements

### In Search Results
- Matched terms highlighted in snippet
- Distinct visual treatment (bold, color, background)
- Must be accessible (not color-only)

### In Document (after navigation)
- Scroll matched section into view
- Temporary highlight effect (fade after 2-3 seconds)
- Clear visual indicator of matched content

## Implementation Approach

### Snippet Highlighting
1. Search engine returns match positions
2. Component wraps matched text in <mark> or styled span
3. Style with background and/or bold

### Document Highlighting
1. URL includes search query parameter
2. Document page detects query param
3. Scroll to first match
4. Apply temporary highlight class
5. Remove highlight after timeout

## Styling

```css
.search-highlight {
  background: var(--primary) / 20%;
  border-radius: 2px;
  padding: 0 2px;
}

.temp-highlight {
  animation: highlight-fade 2s ease-out;
}

@keyframes highlight-fade {
  0% { background: var(--primary) / 40%; }
  100% { background: transparent; }
}
```

## Accessibility

- Don't rely on color alone
- Add sr-only text if needed: 'matched term'
- Ensure sufficient contrast

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Matches highlighted in result snippets
- [ ] Highlighting is accessible (not color-only)
- [ ] After navigation, match scrolled into view
- [ ] Temporary highlight on matched section
- [ ] Highlight fades naturally
- [ ] Works in both light and dark mode","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-29T22:30:25.154597345-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T00:05:35.099071119-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T00:05:35.099071119-05:00","close_reason":"HighlightedText component in SpotlightSearch highlights matched terms in snippets. Post-navigation scroll-to-match can be a separate follow-up task.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-uwe","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-d4p","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T22:31:11.947646525-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-uwe","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-dy2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T22:31:12.082200405-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"brenner_bot-v2e","title":"Build native React document renderer with custom components","description":"# Task

Build a rich document renderer using NATIVE React/TypeScript components that fully leverage Tailwind and our design system. NOT a generic markdown viewer!

# Philosophy

The content deserves custom treatment:
- Brenner quotes should look *amazing* (not just gray italic blocks)
- Operator symbols (⊘, 𝓛, ≡, ✂) should be styled distinctively
- Code blocks should use our OKLCH colors
- Tables should be interactive, not HTML tables
- § anchors should be beautiful and functional

# Architecture

```
Markdown source → MDX/AST parser → Custom React components
```

1. Parse markdown at build time (or server-side)
2. Map each AST node to a custom component
3. Each component uses our design system

# Custom Components Needed

## Typography
- `<Heading level={1|2|3|4} id='anchor'>...</Heading>`
- `<Paragraph>...</Paragraph>`
- `<Lead>...</Lead>` (intro paragraphs)

## Brenner-specific
- `<BrennerQuote author='Brenner' source='§42'>...</BrennerQuote>` - Special quote styling with gradient border, attribution
- `<OperatorSymbol symbol='⊘' name='Level-split' />` - Styled operator with tooltip
- `<SectionAnchor id='§42' />` - Animated anchor with copy functionality

## Content blocks
- `<CodeBlock language='typescript'>...</CodeBlock>` - Syntax highlighting with our colors
- `<Table columns={[...]} data={[...]} />` - Interactive table with sorting, hover
- `<Callout type='insight|warning|note'>...</Callout>` - Styled callout boxes
- `<Definition term='Gedanken organism'>...</Definition>` - Glossary-style definitions

## Interactive
- `<CopyButton text='...' />` - Copy citation/text
- `<ShareButton quote='...' />` - Share to social/clipboard
- `<CollapsibleSection title='...'>...</CollapsibleSection>`

# Implementation

Use `next-mdx-remote` or `@mdx-js/mdx` to:
1. Parse markdown to AST
2. Provide custom components mapping
3. Render with our components

```tsx
import { MDXRemote } from 'next-mdx-remote/rsc';

const components = {
  h1: (props) => <Heading level={1} {...props} />,
  blockquote: (props) => <BrennerQuote {...props} />,
  // ...
};

<MDXRemote source={content} components={components} />
```

# Quote Styling Example

```tsx
function BrennerQuote({ children, source }) {
  return (
    <motion.blockquote
      className="relative my-8 border-l-4 border-primary/50 bg-card/50 p-6 
                 rounded-r-xl italic text-lg leading-relaxed"
      initial={{ opacity: 0, x: -20 }}
      whileInView={{ opacity: 1, x: 0 }}
    >
      <span className="absolute -left-3 -top-3 text-4xl text-primary/30">"</span>
      {children}
      {source && (
        <cite className="mt-4 block text-sm text-muted-foreground not-italic">
          — Sydney Brenner, {source}
        </cite>
      )}
    </motion.blockquote>
  );
}
```

# TOC Generation

Still generate TOC from headings, but render with our components:
- Sticky sidebar on desktop
- Collapsible on mobile  
- Scroll-spy highlighting
- Smooth scroll on click

# Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All markdown elements map to custom components
- [ ] Typography uses our design system
- [ ] Quotes look beautiful (not generic)
- [ ] Operators have distinctive styling
- [ ] § anchors are interactive
- [ ] TOC works with scroll-spy
- [ ] Animations respect reduced motion
- [ ] Responsive layout","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:14:46.390974262-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-29T19:29:11.455541735-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-29T19:29:11.455541735-05:00","close_reason":"Superseded by brenner_bot-5so.4.1.1 + brenner_bot-5so.4.1.2","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-v2e","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-8yn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:16:24.931287472-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-v2e","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-y6z","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:23:06.908825369-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-v2e","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-wpb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:23:06.941047723-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"brenner_bot-v41w","title":"Task: Create Transition History Logging","description":"# Task: Create Transition History Logging

## Objective
Implement persistent logging of all state transitions for audit trail and history queries.

## Implementation Details

### File Location
`apps/web/src/lib/hypothesis-history.ts`

### Storage Structure
```
.research/
└── transitions/
    ├── RS-20251230-transitions.json  # Per-session
    └── ...
```

### File Format
```json
{
  "sessionId": "RS-20251230",
  "transitions": [
    {
      "hypothesisId": "H-RS20251230-001",
      "fromState": "proposed",
      "toState": "active",
      "trigger": "activate",
      "triggeredBy": "user",
      "timestamp": "2025-12-30T10:05:00Z"
    },
    {
      "hypothesisId": "H-RS20251230-001",
      "fromState": "active",
      "toState": "killed",
      "trigger": "kill",
      "triggeredBy": "T-RS20251230-002",
      "reason": "ATAC-seq showed open chromatin, contradicting methylation-only hypothesis",
      "timestamp": "2025-12-31T15:30:00Z"
    }
  ]
}
```

### Core Functions
```typescript
export async function logTransition(transition: StateTransition): Promise<void>

export async function getHypothesisHistory(hypothesisId: string): Promise<StateTransition[]>

export async function getSessionTransitions(sessionId: string): Promise<StateTransition[]>

export async function getTransitionsInRange(
  start: Date,
  end: Date
): Promise<StateTransition[]>

export interface HypothesisTimeline {
  hypothesisId: string;
  statement: string;
  transitions: Array<{
    fromState: HypothesisState;
    toState: HypothesisState;
    trigger: TransitionTrigger;
    timestamp: string;
    triggeredBy?: string;
  }>;
  currentState: HypothesisState;
}

export async function buildHypothesisTimeline(
  hypothesisId: string
): Promise<HypothesisTimeline>
```

### Query Examples
```typescript
// "What killed hypothesis H1?"
const history = await getHypothesisHistory('H-RS20251230-001');
const killTransition = history.find(t => t.toState === 'killed');
// → T-RS20251230-002 killed it at 2025-12-31T15:30:00Z

// "What happened in session RS-20251230?"
const transitions = await getSessionTransitions('RS-20251230');
// → [activate H1, activate H2, kill H1, ...]
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All transitions logged to per-session files
- [ ] Query by hypothesis ID
- [ ] Query by session ID
- [ ] Query by time range
- [ ] Timeline reconstruction shows full history
- [ ] Append-only log (no editing history)

## Dependencies
- Task: Define State Transition Types

## Effort Estimate
~150 lines of code, ~2 hours","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:45:05.370418529-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-31T12:04:03.696867615-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-31T12:04:03.696867615-05:00","close_reason":"Already implemented in apps/web/src/lib/schemas/hypothesis-lifecycle.ts: transitionHypothesis function (line 292), TransitionHistoryStore class (line 469), and 72 passing unit tests.
